working: Email open rates are exceptional: 52.15% avg across 100 campaigns, and every in-window send landed 42-56% (Alumni eNews June 55.71%, Alumni April 53.18%) - far above the 25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark.

Biggest session lifts came from Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (+66.5% lift, 48.53% open) and Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (+65.2% lift, 46.25% open) - staff-client and reminder-style sends move traffic best.
Referral grew +14 sessions vs the 06-22 run (118 -> 132, now 6.4%) and Organic Social grew +9 (35 -> 44, 1.7% -> 2.1%).
Organic Search is holding as a strong #2 channel at 793 sessions (38.6%), nearly matching Direct's share of the 2,056-session window.
not_working: UTM leakage is total: all 8 in-window sends show email_sessions_3d=0 while posting 21-53 direct_sessions_3d - newsletter links carry no UTM tags, so GA4 mis-credits every email click as Direct (Direct's 51.5% / 1,058-session top-channel share is inflated by this).
Click rates are weak despite high opens: most in-window sends clicked 0.4-1.18%; Final Reminder - June managed only 0.69% click / 1.49% CTOR on a 46.25% open - opens are not converting to clicks.
Several sends posted negative lift: Alumni eNews - May 2026 (05-02) -50.2%, Staff-Client eNews - April -15.7%, Alumni eNews - June (05-27) -9.8%.
Organic Social is still under the 5% benchmark at 2.1% (44 sessions), and GSC avg position slipped to 19.86 (from 18.48 on 06-22) with clicks down to 6 (from 8).
do_next: Tag all newsletter links with utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign) so GA4 credits Email instead of Direct - single highest-value fix and it will de-inflate the Direct channel.
Repromote the formats that lift: Staff-Client eNews (+66.5%) and reminder-style Alumni sends (+65.2%) drove the biggest spikes - lean into the staff-client + reminder cadence.
Close the high-open/low-click gap: tighten CTAs and subject-to-content match on Alumni sends (46-56% open but <1.2% click) - this is the biggest conversion leak.
SEO follow-up: avg GSC position slipped to 19.86 from 18.48 with clicks down 8 -> 6 - review the 22 tracked keywords with data for the ones losing ground before the next pulse.
summary: All three data sources (GA4, Constant Contact, GSC) are live. Opens are outstanding (52% avg) and staff-client/reminder sends lift traffic ~65%, but missing UTM tags are routing every newsletter click into Direct, inflating it to 51.5% of the 2,056-session window - adding utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email is the top priority.
